# Copilot Instructions

## Overview

Personal dotfiles for bootstrapping a macOS development environment. The entry point is `main.sh`, which presents an interactive multi-select menu (via `lib/prompt_for_multiselect.sh`) to choose which components to install.

## Running the setup

```bash
# Always run from the repo root
bash main.sh
```

## Architecture

```
.
├── main.sh                        # Orchestrator: interactive menu + dispatches to install functions
├── bootstrap.sh                   # Remote bootstrap: git clone + runs main.sh
├── lib/
│   ├── colors.sh                  # Logging helpers, progress bar, brew_install, track_result
│   ├── ui.sh                      # Banner, prerequisites check, summary, post-install hints
│   ├── doctor.sh                  # --doctor mode: environment health check
│   ├── install_core.sh            # installBrew, installGit, installZsh, installAsdf
│   ├── install_software.sh        # installSoftware* (Pro/Dev/LLM/Tools/Comm/Office/Games)
│   └── prompt_for_multiselect.sh  # Reusable interactive checkbox TUI
├── config/
│   ├── zsh/
│   │   ├── zsh_preload.zsh        # Sourced before OMZ: theme, plugins, p10k instant prompt
│   │   ├── zsh_postload.zsh       # Sourced after OMZ: PATH, aliases, tools (asdf, brew, etc.)
│   │   ├── zsh_aliases.zsh        # Shell aliases and git utility functions
│   │   ├── zsh_custom.zsh         # Template copied once to ~/.zsh_custom.zsh (never overwritten)
│   │   └── p10k.zsh               # Powerlevel10k prompt config
│   ├── asdf/
│   │   ├── tool-versions          # Pinned runtime versions (Node.js, pnpm, Python, Java)
│   │   └── default-npm-packages   # Packages auto-installed with every new Node.js version
│   └── nvim/
│       └── init.vim               # Neovim configuration
└── tests/
    ├── zshrc_config.bats          # Static structural tests for zsh config files
    └── test_helper.bash           # BATS test helpers
```

## Zsh config loading order

```
~/.zshrc (generated by OMZ, modified by installer)
  ├── export DOTFILE_PATH="..."                          # injected — absolute path to repo
  ├── source $DOTFILE_PATH/config/zsh/zsh_preload.zsh   # injected before OMZ load
  ├── source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh
  └── source $DOTFILE_PATH/config/zsh/zsh_postload.zsh  # injected at end of file
```

`DOTFILE_PATH` is written in plain text into `~/.zshrc` at install time. If you move the repo, update this line manually.

## File linking strategy

| File | Strategy | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `zsh_preload.zsh` / `zsh_postload.zsh` | Sourced directly from repo via `$DOTFILE_PATH` | No symlink needed — path injected into `~/.zshrc` |
| `p10k.zsh` | Symlinked → `~/.p10k.zsh` | `p10k configure` writes to `~/.p10k.zsh` by convention |
| `zsh_custom.zsh` | Copied once → `~/.zsh_custom.zsh` | Protected from overwrites; for local-only customization |
| `tool-versions` | Symlinked → `~/.tool-versions` | ASDF reads this path by convention |

## Key conventions

### macOS Apple Silicon priority
`zsh_postload.zsh` exports `/opt/homebrew/bin` first and evaluates `brew shellenv` for ARM.

### Docker setup
Docker runs via **Colima** (not Docker Desktop). Aliases defined in `zsh_postload.zsh`:
```zsh
dockerStart   # starts colima + Portainer UI
dockerStop    # stops colima
dockerUi      # opens http://localhost:9000
```

### ASDF version management
All runtimes (Node.js, pnpm, Python, Java) are managed through ASDF. Versions are pinned in `config/asdf/tool-versions`.

### Adding a new install step
1. Write a function named `installXxx()` in `lib/install_core.sh` or `lib/install_software.sh`
2. Add the label to the semicolon-separated string in the `prompt_for_multiselect` call in `main.sh`
3. Add a `case` branch in the dispatch loop in `main.sh`
